This potion is **NOT** for those who are looking for some sort of affection or love potion...that is, unless you're keen on your suitor trying to maul you to death. When this potion makes contact with the skin of any creature or subject, it will force their sweat glands to secrete fluids that will mix with the stimulant, attracting any non-sapient creatures nearby and causing them to become hostile toward them.    INGREDIENTS:  - Hylek poison (trust me) - Jar of bees (please don't drop it)  - Fresh stomach (don't ask from where)  - Spiky fruit

This potion must be kept at room temperature **at all times**. No exceptions unless you're eager to be rid of a leg. Keep stored within a well-ventilated and dehumidified area. Do not keep past expiration date noted on the bottom of the container, and do not jostle the container as the liquid inside is volatile. Most critically, under no circumstances can you drop this vial unless you're prepared to meet the Six (or whatever omnipotent being you may seek guidance from).    INGREDIENTS:  - Otherworldly ooze  - A tooth of something big  - Hylek poison  - Moon-touched flower

This precedent was established in the High Court and is set forth due to the discourse of one the finest scholars in Ahdashim. Her treatise, "Life of the Ley Line," has brought new findings to light regarding ley line energy. Specifically, her results prove to be unique to our ley line and ours alone...Because of this, we feel we must close Ahdashim to the outside world to protect our magic...as evil lurks, and not from afar.

This skull was retrieved from Arah following Zhaitan's defeat in 1326 AE, and it is believed to have belonged to a remnant of their species who dwelled in the city shortly after its founding. It is unclear how they survived; magical stasis is one of several possibilities.    Zhaitan called this individual's Risen corpse from a hidden burial site that contained a collection of personal effects and grave relics. Preparations have been made to return the bones and artifacts to Arah after they are catalogued and confirmed safe for reburial.

This was it—the final push. Braham would claim glory for his legend, Rox would earn a place in the Stone warband, and Marjory and Kasmeer would exact justice for the whole of Kryta. We found Scarlet Briar wounded in front of a central control console for the entire operation. She ranted and raved at us, mocking our heroics and gloating about what was to come: that Tyria would serve a new master. Marjory and Braham moved in to apprehend her, but the crafty engineer still had one last trick up her sleeve. Marjory and Braham took the brunt of Scarlet's trap, getting thrown back by a concussive force and lightning. Rox ordered Braham to stay put as she treated his broken leg, refusing to leave his side. Marjory appeared far worse off. She was lying on the floor motionless, bruised, and burned. Kasmeer tried to wake her, but there was no response. The mesmer's expression darkened and she rose to her feet, staff trembling in hand. Enraged, she cried out and sprinted back toward the fight.  Scarlet was ready with a stockpile of grenades, but it couldn't hold the two of us at bay for long. She became distracted by Kasmeer's illusions just long enough for the opening I needed to deal a fatal blow. With her last words, Scarlet said her death wouldn't save us. I glanced at the main screen of the console behind her. We were too late; the drill was about to breach its intended target, the ley-line hub beneath Lion's Arch. Kasmeer had returned to Marjory who, despite Rox's efforts, was still unresponsive. Cradled in Kasmeer's arms, the detective slowly stirred awake. The two embraced and shared a touching moment. Seconds later, the Breachmaker shook as a blinding column of light shot up into the center of the fortress. As the shaking grew in intensity, we hurried our injured friends back through the portal and down to the city. The Breachmaker collapsed shortly after, debris crashing below and littering the whole of Lion's Arch.

Hopefully they will learn from this experience and continue to trade peacefully with each other for years to come.As an added bonus, the runic bloodstones that we received will go a long way toward our efforts in reading the ancient jotun languages. We just might decipher the ancient jotun texts yet!—Albin ChroniclerDurmand Priory

Though Olaf Olafson was a popular figure amongst the norn community of  Drizzlewood, his success was at times a source of jealousy. One specific incident involves norn farmer and former Olafson friend Ike Ikeson. Ikeson frequented Olafson's gatherings, even attending several dinners hosted by the Olafson family. However, after hearing rumors of Olafson hiding his valuables in an undisclosed vault, Ikeson's greed overtook him. He strategically attended Olafson social events with the goal of siphoning clues about the treasure's whereabouts. After a neighbor informed Olafson that he caught Ikeson trying to break into the barn housing his sword, Olafson alleviated the problem by stabbing Ikeson through the abdomen with it, thereby damaging the friendship.
